Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Look up contact details for Samira Tariq, Elena Rostova, and Marcus Thorne using contacts_search_contacts to get their email addresses for the summary email.
- Search emails for ExpenseBot pilot findings using search_emails with queries like "ExpenseBot", "audit", "stipend" or similar to find Dmitri's audit email to Marcus, config issue threads, and receipt investigation correspondence.
- Search Slack for pilot discussions using conversations_search_messages with queries like "ExpenseBot", "pilot", "config" or similar to find root cause analysis in engineering and financial escalations.
- Search Linear for ExpenseBot pilot issues using linear_list_issues filtered by the ExpenseBot project or queries like "ExpenseBot", "audit", "config" or similar to find config fixes, audit tickets, duplicate detection, and pipeline hardening work.
- Search QuickBooks for stipend payment holds using quickbooks_search_bills with queries like "stipend", "hold", "STIP" or similar to find the admin holds Marcus placed on pending reimbursements and the already paid Kevin Tran receipt.
- Search Airtable stipend records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on the stipends table for TechComfort Supply entries across all client accounts to confirm whether additional suspicious receipts exist beyond the 3 already flagged.
- Send email via send_email from dmitri.volkov@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com and elena.rostova@moveops.com, CC marcus.thorne@moveops.com, with the compiled post mortem summary covering configs, financial exposure, and receipt audit status.
- Update the pilot audit issue to done/completed state via linear_update_issue.
- Add a closing comment on the audit issue via linear_create_comment linking config findings, financial holds, and TechComfort receipt results together.
- Search Linear for incomplete engineering team tasks from the last 3 months using linear_list_issues filtered by assignees (Samira, Lena, Dmitri, Anh) and states not done, created after approximately January 2026.

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
- Look up contact details for Samira Tariq, Elena Rostova, and Marcus Thorne using contacts_search_contacts to get their email addresses for the summary email.
- Search emails for ExpenseBot pilot findings using search_emails with queries like "ExpenseBot", "audit", "stipend" or similar to find Dmitri's audit email to Marcus, config issue threads, and receipt investigation correspondence.
- Search Slack for pilot discussions using conversations_search_messages with queries like "ExpenseBot", "pilot", "config" or similar to find root cause analysis in engineering and financial escalations.
- Search Linear for ExpenseBot pilot issues using linear_list_issues filtered by the ExpenseBot project or queries like "ExpenseBot", "audit", "config" or similar to find config fixes, audit tickets, duplicate detection, and pipeline hardening work.
- Search QuickBooks for stipend payment holds using quickbooks_search_bills with queries like "stipend", "hold", "STIP" or similar to find the admin holds Marcus placed on pending reimbursements and the already paid Kevin Tran receipt.
- Search Airtable stipend records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on the stipends table for TechComfort Supply entries across all client accounts to confirm whether additional suspicious receipts exist beyond the 3 already flagged.
- Send email via send_email from dmitri.volkov@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com and elena.rostova@moveops.com, CC marcus.thorne@moveops.com, with the compiled post mortem summary covering configs, financial exposure, and receipt audit status.
- Update the pilot audit issue to done/completed state via linear_update_issue.
- Add a closing comment on the audit issue via linear_create_comment linking config findings, financial holds, and TechComfort receipt results together.
- Search Linear for incomplete engineering team tasks from the last 3 months using linear_list_issues filtered by assignees (Samira, Lena, Dmitri, Anh) and states not done, created after approximately January 2026.

12 The post mortem email (sent via send_email) mentions the ExpenseBot configuration bugs, specifically referencing either the Vectral silent fallback to default template limits (e.g., $400 default instead of the custom $600 ergonomic limit) or the Mosaic malformed JSON types (e.g., max_amount stored as string, excluded_categories as string instead of array), or both.
outcome
 —✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
—Run #1No justification
✓Run #2The post-mortem email explicitly describes Vectral's silent fallback to default template limits (e.g., Ergonomic set to $400 instead of $600) and Mosaic's malformed JSON types (excluded_categories as string, max_amount as string causing lexicographic comparison).
✓Run #3The post-mortem email explicitly describes the Vectral silent fallback to default template limits (Ergonomic set to $400 instead of the correct $600) and the Mosaic malformed JSON types (excluded_categories stored as string 'coworking' instead of array, max_amount stored as '800' string instead of number 800). Both config bugs are clearly described.
✓Run #4The post-mortem email body explicitly describes both config bugs: Vectral's 'silent fallback to default template limits' (citing the $400 vs $600 ergonomic discrepancy) and Mosaic's 'malformed JSON types' (max_amount as string '800', excluded_categories as string instead of array).
✓Run #5The email body explicitly describes both the Vectral silent fallback issue ($400 default vs $600 correct ergonomic limit) and the Mosaic malformed JSON types (excluded_categories as string instead of array, max_amount as string '800' instead of number 800).
✓Run #6The post-mortem email explicitly describes both the Vectral silent fallback to default limits (catch block swallowed error, fell back to default template) and the Mosaic malformed JSON types (excluded_categories as string, max_amount as string '800' instead of number 800).

outcome
